default.$description: Default canvas and page background — the base layer most content sits on.

overlay.$description: Overlay background — use for modal sheets, dialogs, and panel surfaces that float above the canvas.

inverted.$description: Inverted background — dark in light mode, light in dark mode. Use for tooltips and high-contrast popovers.

default.$description: Primary brand background — use for filled primary buttons and high-emphasis interactive elements.

subtle.$description: Subtle primary background — use for low-emphasis brand containers, selected rows, and callout surfaces.

muted.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

elevated.$description: Elevated primary background — use for darkest brand surfaces requiring maximum contrast.

deep.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

default.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

elevated.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

default.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

elevated.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

muted.$description: Muted success background — use for success message containers and confirmation banners.

elevated.$description: Elevated success background — use for filled success badges and positive status chips.
Elevated warning background — use for filled warning badges and caution status chips.

muted.$description: Semantic background color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

subtle.$description: Subtle destructive background — use for error message containers and inline error banners.

muted.$description: Muted destructive background — use for moderate-emphasis error containers.

elevated.$description: Elevated destructive background — use for filled destructive action buttons.

deep.$description: Deep destructive background — darkest error surface, use for maximum-contrast destructive elements.

muted.$description: Muted disabled background — use for disabled input fields and inactive form elements.

elevated.$description: Elevated disabled background — use for disabled button fills.

1.$description: Muted background level 1 — subtle off-canvas tint for section differentiation and sidebar fills.

2.$description: Muted background level 2 — secondary surface for cards, dropdowns, and nested containers.

3.$description: Muted background level 3 — tertiary surface for hover states on muted backgrounds.

default.$description: Default body text and primary icon color.

inverted.$description: Inverted foreground — use for text and icons placed on dark or brand-filled surfaces.

focus.$description: Focus indicator foreground — use for focus ring fills on interactive elements.

muted.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

elevated.$description: Primary brand foreground — use for brand-colored links, active nav items, and emphasis icons.

$root.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

brand.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

green.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

amber.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

red.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

blue.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

neutral.$description: Semantic foreground color for text and icon treatments. Use this when intent matters more than palette choice.

muted.$description: Muted success foreground — use for low-emphasis success copy on subtle success surfaces.

elevated.$description: Success foreground — use for confirmation text and positive status icon fills.

muted.$description: Muted warning foreground — use for low-emphasis warning copy on subtle warning surfaces.

elevated.$description: Warning foreground — use for warning text and caution icon fills.

muted.$description: Muted destructive foreground — use for low-emphasis error copy on subtle destructive surfaces.

elevated.$description: Destructive foreground — use for error text, delete action labels, and critical icon fills.

muted.$description: Muted disabled foreground — use for disabled input text and inactive labels.

elevated.$description: Elevated disabled foreground — use for disabled button labels and inactive interactive elements.

1.$description: Muted foreground level 1 — secondary text, supporting labels, and secondary icons.

2.$description: Muted foreground level 2 — tertiary text, placeholder copy, and hint text.

3.$description: Muted foreground level 3 — lowest-emphasis text, timestamps, and secondary metadata.

default.$description: Default border — general-purpose dividers, input outlines, and card edges.

faint.$description: Semantic border color for outlines, dividers, focus, and stateful strokes.

muted.$description: Muted border — subtle dividers between low-contrast surfaces.

elevated.$description: Elevated border — stronger dividers for prominent section breaks and elevated cards.

focus.$description: Focus ring border — visible focus outline applied to interactive elements on keyboard navigation.

primary.$description: Primary border — brand-tinted outline for selected or active primary elements.

default.$description: Success border — outline for success-state inputs and confirmation containers.
Default destructive border — outline for error-state inputs and destructive form containers.

muted.$description: Muted destructive border — subtle outline for low-emphasis error indicators.

elevated.$description: Elevated destructive border — stronger outline for high-emphasis error state elements.

muted.$description: Semantic border color for outlines, dividers, focus, and stateful strokes.

elevated.$description: Warning border — outline for warning-state inputs and caution containers.

muted.$description: Semantic border color for outlines, dividers, focus, and stateful strokes.

elevated.$description: Semantic border color for outlines, dividers, focus, and stateful strokes.

default.$description: Semantic surface color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

elevated.$description: Semantic surface color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

hover.$description: Semantic surface color for surfaces and states. Alias this token in components instead of using primitive colors directly.

thin.$description: Semantic border width for thin strokes (1px). Use on subtle borders and dividers.

default.$description: Semantic border width for default strokes (1.5px). Standard border thickness for most components.

medium.$description: Semantic border width for medium strokes (2px). Use on interactive or emphasized borders.

thick.$description: Semantic border width for thick strokes (3px). Use for strong visual separators.

heavy.$description: Semantic border width for heavy strokes (4px). Use for prominent or decorative borders.

color.$description: Focus ring color for primary state.

blur.$description: Focus ring blur radius for primary state.

spread.$description: Focus ring spread for primary state.

offset-x.$description: Focus ring horizontal offset for primary state.

offset-y.$description: Focus ring vertical offset for primary state.

sm.$description: Breakpoint for tablet portrait and up (768px).

md.$description: Breakpoint for tablet landscape and up (1024px).

xs.$description: Container max-width for tooltips, dropdowns, narrow popovers (320px).

sm.$description: Container max-width for cards, small modals, side panels (480px).

md.$description: Container max-width for forms, medium modals, content cards (640px).

lg.$description: Container max-width for large modals, content sections (768px).

xl.$description: Container max-width for page containers, wide layouts (1024px).

2xl.$description: Container max-width for full-width content cap (1280px).
